title: Separate setup helpers from VCS shim
rationale: Dispatcher setup/install behavior is stack-specific setup logic rather than VCS branching logic, so it should live outside the git compatibility shim.

name: Tighten QA/finalize validation loops
description: Developer prompt now forbids unrelated test copying, QA reports must include npm test command/count evidence, and finalize distinguishes modified-file failures from pre-existing unrelated failures so branch-ready is blocked without wasting a developer retry.

name: Finalize phase outputs
description: Finalize now uses FINALIZE_VALIDATION.md as the workflow verdict artifact while still writing FINALIZE_REPORT.md for operational push/rebase details.

title: Refinery jj rebases must stay on the backend path
rationale: Refinery previously ran raw git rebase <target> <branch> from the repo root even in colocated jj repos, which broke the branch-ready invariant by validating one merge path in finalize and a different path in refinery. Rebased jj runs should go through VcsBackend.rebase() in the run workspace so finalize and refinery share backend semantics.

title: Finalize failure must not infer success from missing mail
rationale: agent-worker onPipelineComplete previously treated missing finalize phase-complete/agent-error mail as success, which allowed finalize FAIL verdicts to enqueue merge work and reach refinery. The worker must preserve the pipeline success=false result when finalize mail is absent.

name: Keep orchestrator tests aligned with runtime validation contracts
description: Pipeline epic tests need QA fixture reports with npm test command text and pass/fail counts so qaReportHasTestEvidence() treats PASS/FAIL verdicts as valid. Refinery migration tests should assert backend mergeWithoutCommit()/getConflictingFiles() behavior rather than raw git --squash shell calls.

title: Ignore detached HEAD branch labels for dispatch and merge targeting
rationale: Detached workspaces can surface HEAD as the current branch, but branch:HEAD is not a real merge target. Rejecting it at branch-label extraction/application prevents dispatcher from persisting stale branch:HEAD labels and makes refinery fall back to the real default or explicit target branch instead of failing the no-commits guard against HEAD.

title: Skip branch-ready when troubleshooter already landed the branch
rationale: A finalize failure can still be recoverable by the troubleshooter, but if that recovery directly lands the work on the target branch, enqueueing the run to merge queue and emitting branch-ready causes a false no-commits merge failure. After troubleshooter recovery, agent-worker should detect zero diff versus the target branch, close the bead, and skip merge queue / branch-ready.

content: Troubleshooter prompts and tool-facing JSON should use bead/beadId terminology. Keep legacy seedId only as a compatibility alias for internal runtime fields and older tool callers, not as the primary language shown to agents or operators.

description: In colocated jj repos, JujutsuBackend.detectDefaultBranch() and getCurrentBranch() cannot rely on main/master or raw working-copy change IDs. During a dirty working copy on dev, getCurrentBranch() returned the unbookmarked jj change ID (for example yozrpnkmlxpq), and run/auto-merge threaded that into merge targeting. Refinery then reported no-commits against the change ID instead of the real trunk.
resolution: Honor git-town.main-branch and origin/HEAD before bookmark-name fallbacks in detectDefaultBranch(), and in getCurrentBranch() fall back to the parent revision's bookmark before returning a jj change ID.

title: Finalize pre-existing test failures should reconcile to merged when already landed
rationale: A finalize verdict of tests_failed_pre_existing_issues does not necessarily mean the bead failed to land. In jj/git workflows the branch may already match the target branch by the time finalize reports the deterministic failure. In that case agent-worker should mark the run merged and close the bead instead of recording a failed run that contradicts repository state.

description: Finalize retry suppression depended on parseFinalizeFailureScope(), but the parser only recognized inline '## Failure Scope: ...' or plain '- UNRELATED_FILES' formatting. Real finalize artifacts used section formatting with bold bullets ('## Failure Scope' then '- **UNRELATED_FILES**'), so the parser returned unknown and pipeline-executor incorrectly sent Finalize Feedback - Retry N back to developer.
resolution: Accept bold bullet section formatting in parseFinalizeFailureScope() so UNRELATED_FILES is recognized from real finalize artifacts and finalize does not re-enter the developer/QA loop for pre-existing unrelated failures.

title: Finalize only reruns validation on target drift
rationale: QA already validated the bead; finalize should only rerun npm test when the target branch revision changed after QA. Persisting QA target/head refs in run progress lets finalize distinguish no-drift pushes from true drift verification.

title: Finalize integration uses intent-based command naming
rationale: The finalize step is meant to bring target-branch drift onto the bead branch for validation, not to prescribe rebase as the architectural policy. Renaming FinalizeCommands.rebaseCommand to integrateTargetCommand and the prompt variable to vcsIntegrateTargetCommand keeps backend behavior flexible while matching finalize's real purpose.

title: Finalize skip-vs-rerun is runtime-enforced
rationale: Pipeline-executor now records the current target ref before finalize verdict handling and validates FINALIZE_VALIDATION.md against the drift expectation. If target refs match, finalize must mark Test Validation as SKIPPED; if target drift exists, finalize may not skip validation. This prevents prompt drift or agent mistakes from bypassing the finalize drift contract.

title: JJ head resolution should ignore ephemeral scratch children
rationale: In colocated jj repos, bookmark moves and git-imports can leave the default workspace on an unbookmarked empty child commit while the real branch tip sits on @-. JujutsuBackend.getHeadId() now prefers the parent change when @ is unbookmarked but @- carries bookmarks, so finalize/refinery runtime checks reason about the effective branch tip instead of the scratch working-copy child.
